title: Applying Cross-Encoder Reranking
concept: Passing the top merged candidates through a cross-encoder to re-score them for relevance.

exercises.prompt: What does RRF stand for in hybrid search setup?
exercises.explain: RRF stands for Ranked Retrieval Fusion, and it is used to combine results from different search methods.

cards.title: Cross-Encoder Reranking
cards.body: Cross-encoder reranking is a method to improve the relevance of search results. It scores candidate answers in a more detailed way.

cards.title: What is a Cross-Encoder?
cards.body: A cross-encoder is a model that processes multiple pieces of information together and assigns a relevance score to them.

cards.title: Like a Movie Critic
cards.body: Imagine a movie critic who reviews the best films together. Their final review is more nuanced than if they just reviewed each film separately.

cards.title: Example of Reranking
cards.body: If a search returns five top candidates, passing them through a cross-encoder allows us to find which one really answers the question best.

cards.title: Myth: All Search Results are Equal
cards.body: Many believe that top search results are always the best. In reality, reranking can reveal the true relevance of candidates.

cards.title: Cross-Encoder vs. Standard Scoring
cards.body: Here’s how cross-encoder reranking enhances relevance:

left.text: Gives basic scores, may miss nuances.

right.text: Gives detailed relevance scores by considering context.

exercises.prompt: What does a cross-encoder do during reranking?

options.text: It processes multiple candidates together for improved scoring.
It combines results without evaluating them.
It only ranks the top result.

exercises.explain: A cross-encoder evaluates multiple candidates together, which leads to better scoring of their relevance to a query.

exercises.prompt: Cross-encoder reranking cannot improve the quality of search results.
exercises.explain: False! Cross-encoder reranking definitely improves the quality of search results by providing more detailed evaluation.

exercises.prompt: Cross-encoders evaluate candidates by processing them together for improved ___ scoring.
exercises.sentence: Cross-encoders evaluate candidates by processing them together for improved relevance scoring.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'relevance', which highlights that cross-encoders focus on how well the candidates relate to the query.

exercises.prompt: Which statement best describes the importance of cross-encoder reranking?

options.text: It eliminates the need for hybrid search.
It improves the precision of answer relevance from candidates.
It is faster than standard scoring methods.

exercises.explain: The correct statement is that cross-encoder reranking improves the precision of answer relevance, ensuring the best candidates are highlighted.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of using a cross-encoder for reranking candidates after a hybrid search?

options.text: It speeds up the search process.
It provides a less nuanced score than basic scoring.
It allows for a detailed analysis of candidates for accurate scoring.
It replaces the need for initial candidate selection.

exercises.explain: The main benefit of using a cross-encoder is that it allows for a detailed analysis of candidates, leading to accurate scoring based on context and relevance.
